title: cyphostemma griseorubrum
intro: Cyphostemma griseorubrum, also known as the African potato or bushman's poison, is a plant species in the grape family (Vitaceae) that is native to southern Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cyphostemma griseorubrum include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and made into a decoction or infusion for use in traditional medicine.
